title: Critokian

'''Critokians''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of two-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-tall, [[Spider/Legends|arachnoid]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] from the [[Critoki system/Legends|Critoki system]], although many dwelt in tribes on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. They used large claws to [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]] prey on the jungle world, and several of their number eventually departed Yavin 4 and found work as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]. The Critokians' biological makeup featured a complex [[nervous system]] and six holes though which they could both communicate and breathe.

==Biology and appearance==
Critokians were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings, two [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall,<ref name="daa" /> [[Spider/Legends|arachnoid]]<ref name="mf" /> yet bipedal, with four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]. Their fingers were tentacle-like in nature, an arrangement that allowed them to manipulate objects. Members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] possessed large claws and could both run fast and swim at a rapid pace. Each Critokian had six holes on the forehead that permitted respiration on both land and [[Water/Legends|water]], as well as facilitating communication. The upper portion of their main body, or head, housed the complex [[nervous system]]. The lower portion contained the Critokian [[Heart/Legends|heart]], as well as the circulatory and digestive systems. The skin of a Critokian was [[Color/Legends|yellow]], although it also featured black coloring at its joints, as well as at the base and tip of the body. Each member of the species had two, red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]: one on either side, slightly above the point at which the larger limbs joined with the main body.<ref name="daa" />

==Society and culture==
The Critokians hailed from the [[Critoki system/Legends|Critoki system]]<ref name="tea" /><ref name="jcf" /> and lived in tribal societies on the jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. With their large claws, they were able to kill prey. In order to communicate, they used a [[Critokian language|series of hoots and whistles]].<ref name="daa" />

==History==
The Critokians came from the Critoki system,<ref name="tea" /><ref name="jcf" /> which became part of the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}.</ref> within the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] reaches<ref name="tea" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="EAOC" /> sometime between [[3000 BBY|3000]] and [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. Their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] fell within contested [[Realspace/Legends|space]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of c. [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="tea" /> then fell within [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]'s [[Zsinj's Empire|territory]] from [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] until [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]];<ref name="tea" /> it then lay within the contested Borderland Regions during the [[Thrawn campaign]] before falling within the bounds of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire]] by [[137 ABY]].<ref name="tea" /> At some point in their history, those [[Alien/Legends|Non-Humans]] also established their presence on Yavin 4,<ref name="daa" /> one of the three habitable satellites of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 2">''[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]]''</ref>

==Critokians in the galaxy==
Several Critokians found their way off Critoki and Yavin 4 to become skillful [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]].<ref name="daa" /> At [[Sok Brok's Fiftieth Annual Pet Show]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], visitors could encounter an arachnoid Critokian walking a bipedal [[ornuk]] on a leash. That sight, if admittedly comical, was not considered uncommon.<ref name="mf" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Ooi Say Hien]], at the age of 13, created the Critokian species through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition held in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine. The contest was launched in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]), and the winners were published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]). Hien's entry was the winner for the "Under 16 years old" subdivision of the contest. Continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] later stated that published "Design an Alien" material was to be considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> The Critokians were subsequently referenced by [[James Luceno]] in his novel [[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] ([[2008]]), making them one of the two "Design an Alien" alien species to be directly referenced in a later work—the other being the [[Skel]]s of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. In ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]), authors [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] established the world of Critoki. Fry had drawn the reference from ''Millennium Falcon'' and was unaware of the species' origins in "Design an Alien." Although the source did not directly establish Critoki as the original homeworld of the species before they potentially emigrated to Yavin 4, Fry has declared that it would "make more sense that way."<ref name="jcf" />
